organizations.role: Public supporter / donor (self-stated pro-Israel political interest)
organizations.notes: Loewy Law Firm leadership bio and Texas Hillel alumni spotlight both state AIPAC is among organizations the Loewys actively support. Adam has "keen interest in pro-Israel politics and is involved with AIPAC." Disambiguation: Velasquez batch Loewy, Adam / law loewy / Austin 78731 = Adam Jacob Loewy, Loewy Law Firm P.C.

organizations.role: Local AIPAC Leadership Chair
organizations.notes: TIER 1 finding. Identified publicly as Austins Local AIPAC Leadership Chair. Credited with "growing Austins pro-Israel political group." Spoke at Shalom Austin Stand With Israel community events.

organizations.role: "My AIPAC Story" featured speaker (national video series)
organizations.notes: Featured in AIPACs official "My AIPAC Story" video testimonial series

organizations.role: Past Board Chair (4th Chair); National Commissioner; Austin Advisory Board; 2023 Golden Door Awards honoree
organizations.notes: Profile on Revere CRE explicitly lists ADL Austin Past Board Member, Past Chair, National Commissioner, Austin Advisory Board. Confirmed by Shalom Austin Sep 2023 piece ("Choosing Hope in a World of Hate") noting Jeff served as ADL Austin's 4th board chair. Honored with Val Newberg at 2023 ADL Austin Golden Door Awards Dinner (Oct 30, 2023). Disambiguation: Velasquez donor employer "Endeavor Real Estate Group" matches Newberg's role as co-founder/Managing Principal of Endeavor.

organizations.role: Past Board Chair (4th Chair); National Commissioner; Austin Advisory Board; 2023 Golden Door Awards honoree
organizations.notes: Revere CRE profile lists Jeff Newberg as ADL Austin Past Board Member, Past Chair, National Commissioner, Austin Advisory Board. Confirmed by Shalom Austin Sep 2023 "Choosing Hope in a World of Hate" piece noting Jeff served as ADL Austin's 4th board chair, and 2023 ADL Austin Golden Door Awards Dinner honoree (Oct 30, 2023) with wife Val. Disambiguation: Fuentes donor employer "endeavor estate group real" (Endeavor Real Estate Group) and Austin 78746 location match Newberg's role as co-founder/Managing Principal of Endeavor.

organizations.role: Past Board Chair (4th Chair); National Commissioner; 2023 Golden Door Awards honoree
organizations.notes: Shalom Austin Sep 2023 ("Choosing Hope in a World of Hate") confirms Jeff served as ADL Austin's 4th board chair and that he and Val Newberg were honored at the 2023 Golden Door Awards Dinner (Oct 30, 2023). Disambiguation: donor employer "endeavor estate group real" and 78746 Austin address match Jeff Newberg, Endeavor Real Estate Group co-founder/Managing Principal. Same individual canonically listed as "Newberg, Jeff" in other campaigns.

organizations.role: Past Board Chair (4th); National Commissioner
organizations.notes: Per Shalom Austin 2023; also Endeavor Real Estate co-founder

organizations.role: Past Board Chair (2009-2012); helped create Austin Hate Crimes Task Force; expanded No Place for Hate program
organizations.notes: Wikipedia biography of Steve Adler (58th Austin mayor 2015-2023) explicitly documents he "served as the board chair of the Anti-Defamation League Austin Region" from 2009 to 2012 and notes ADL board-chair work alongside Texas Tribune/Ballet Austin civic roles. Disambiguation: Fuentes donor listed as "mayor" at Austin 78701 matches Steve Adler, former Austin mayor.

organizations.role: Founding Board Member; Board Chair 2009-2012
organizations.notes: Helped establish the Austin ADL chapter. Per PolitiFact 2014: led board 2009-2012. Per Forward: "He made ADL the go-to civil rights organization in Austin" — Kirk Rudy quote.

organizations.role: True Colors Vision in Action Award Honoree (2016)
organizations.notes: Co-founder of Notley. Celebrated alongside Lisa Graham for advancing social justice and civil rights.

organizations.role: True Colors Honoree (2016)
organizations.notes: Co-founder of Notley. Honored alongside Dan Graham at ADL Austins 5th annual True Colors: Shake the Hate event.

organizations.role: Longtime Board Member; 2023 Golden Door Awards honoree
organizations.notes: Shalom Austin Sep 2023 piece describes Val Newberg as "a longtime ADL board member and dedicated volunteer." Honored with husband Jeff Newberg at 2023 ADL Austin Golden Door Awards Dinner. Disambiguation: same 78746 Austin address as Jeff Newberg (Endeavor); listed as "homemaker" in Velasquez filing.

organizations.role: Longtime Board Member; 2023 Golden Door Awards honoree

organizations.role: Secretary
organizations.notes: Texas Book Festival board bio explicitly identifies Marc Winkelman as "secretary of the National Jewish Democratic Council". NJDC is a Democratic-aligned Jewish pro-Israel political organization. Disambiguation: Fuentes Winkelman, Marc / calendar executive services / Austin 78744 = Marc Winkelman, CEO Calendar Club / Calendar Holdings (Austin HQ).

organizations.role: Vice President - Southern Operations Manager (2019-2022, retired); ~39-year Exxon/ExxonMobil career in oil and gas engineering and midstream operations
organizations.notes: Johnita (Davis) Jones was appointed Vice President - Southern Operations Manager of ExxonMobil Pipeline Company in 2019 and served until retiring in February 2022; her LinkedIn headline reads 'RETIRED Vice President / Southern...' and lists Austin, TX, which matches the filed occupation string 'Vice President / Southern Operations Manager' with employer 'Retired' almost verbatim, and the March 2022 gift falls one month after her retirement. She earned a chemical engineering degree from Texas A&M in 1983 and spent her career at Exxon/ExxonMobil: facilities engineer with Exxon Company U.S.A. in Louisiana on oil-and-gas assignments (corrosion, project engineering, reservoir analysis), supervisory roles from 1990, Pipeline Risk and Integrity Manager 2001-2016, then Midstream Americas Engineering Manager in 2016. She now serves as a Director of The Association of Former Students (Texas A&M's alumni association). Coded Energy / Environment for the oil-and-gas pipeline career. Note her FEC lean of 0.0 across 2 gifts is Republican-leaning, making this Watson gift cross-partisan.

organizations.org: Austin Women in Oil and Gas
organizations.role: Administrative Chair and Board of Directors (2019-present)
organizations.notes: Partner at Holland & Knight (formerly Thompson & Knight); practice covers oil & gas regulatory and transactional matters in Eagle Ford, Bakken, Permian Basin

organizations.org: Kay Bailey Hutchison Center for Energy, Law & Business (UT Austin)
organizations.role: Advisory Council member (2015-present)
organizations.notes: Energy law and business advisory council at UT Austin

organizations.org: MTG Management, Inc. (d/b/a Major Brand Gasoline)
organizations.role: President - Austin wholesale gasoline/diesel distribution company, ~30 years in operation, 3267 Bee Caves Rd
organizations.notes: Corporationwiki lists Guy Oliver as president of MTG Management, Inc. at 3267 Bee Caves Rd, Austin 78746 - the same address as V&S Enterprises, LC (3267 Bee Cave Rd Ste 107), the employer he self-reported, and Vicki H. Oliver (president/director of V&S Enterprises) is listed as sharing companies with him. His self-reported occupation 'Real Estate' matches V&S Enterprises' recorded activity as an Austin property owner and general contractor. Medium rather than high because no source names Guy Oliver as an officer of V&S Enterprises itself; the link runs through the shared address and the shared associate. AI-generated aggregator profiles (Elementix) claim he holds 36-108 properties across 13 holding companies with $330M-$679M in mortgage exposure, but the figures are mutually inconsistent across their own pages and were not relied on.

organizations.org: MTG Management, Inc. (dba Major Brand Gasoline)
organizations.role: President/owner of a wholesale gasoline distributor and intrastate hazmat fuel carrier — downstream motor-fuel distribution, not upstream exploration/production
organizations.notes: Both employer strings in the filing resolve to real Austin entities Guy Oliver runs, and both sit in the donor's exact zip: MTG Management, Inc. operates under the name 'Major Brand Gasoline' at 3267 Bee Caves Rd, Austin TX 78746 (SIC: Gasoline, Wholesale Trade – Nondurable Goods) with Guy Oliver listed as President, corroborating the filing's 'Senior Managemnt'/'Mtg Management Inc'; it also holds USDOT #2106997 as an intrastate hazmat motor carrier running 7 tank trucks, consistent with fuel hauling. Gozig Holdings, LLC is at 3201 Bee Caves Rd, Austin TX 78746, lists Guy Oliver as Member, and appears in the Texas Food and Fuel Association member directory under 'Petroleum Distributor', corroborating 'Manager'/'GoZig Holdings'. Zip 78746 (West Lake Hills) matches both company addresses. This resolves the 'Mtg Management' ambiguity: it is NOT mortgage management and not the M T G Management Consultants at 111 Congress Ave — the 'Major Brand Gasoline' trade name, gasoline SIC code, and hazmat tanker fleet rule that reading out. Caveat on the label: Oliver also has a substantial real estate side — public-record signature data ties him to ~13 entities (JAYO Interests LLC, JAYO2 LP, 38 12 Street LC, Gozig Holdings LLC), and the City of Austin lobbyist registry lists Gozig Holdings as a 'property owner' client retaining land-use lobbyists from 2017 through 2026. Energy / Environment was chosen because both employers named on the filing itself are fuel/petroleum businesses; Real Estate would be a defensible secondary.

organizations.role: Member; company listed as 'Petroleum Distributor' in the Texas Food and Fuel Association directory
organizations.notes: Both employer strings in the filing resolve to real Austin entities Guy Oliver runs, and both sit in the donor's exact zip: MTG Management, Inc. operates under the name 'Major Brand Gasoline' at 3267 Bee Caves Rd, Austin TX 78746 (SIC: Gasoline, Wholesale Trade – Nondurable Goods) with Guy Oliver listed as President, corroborating the filing's 'Senior Managemnt'/'Mtg Management Inc'; it also holds USDOT #2106997 as an intrastate hazmat motor carrier running 7 tank trucks, consistent with fuel hauling. Gozig Holdings, LLC is at 3201 Bee Caves Rd, Austin TX 78746, lists Guy Oliver as Member, and appears in the Texas Food and Fuel Association member directory under 'Petroleum Distributor', corroborating 'Manager'/'GoZig Holdings'. Zip 78746 (West Lake Hills) matches both company addresses. This resolves the 'Mtg Management' ambiguity: it is NOT mortgage management and not the M T G Management Consultants at 111 Congress Ave — the 'Major Brand Gasoline' trade name, gasoline SIC code, and hazmat tanker fleet rule that reading out. Caveat on the label: Oliver also has a substantial real estate side — public-record signature data ties him to ~13 entities (JAYO Interests LLC, JAYO2 LP, 38 12 Street LC, Gozig Holdings LLC), and the City of Austin lobbyist registry lists Gozig Holdings as a 'property owner' client retaining land-use lobbyists from 2017 through 2026. Energy / Environment was chosen because both employers named on the filing itself are fuel/petroleum businesses; Real Estate would be a defensible secondary.

organizations.role: mineral/royalty owner — commodity type unconfirmed (Central-TX interests may be limestone/aggregate rather than oil & gas)
organizations.notes: Kathleen Irvin Loughlin is owner/partner/founder of Rancho Viejo Ventures and related LLCs (CKI Rancho Viejo Broadway/Riverview) and lists her occupation as rancher/ranch owner; public records place her in Austin 78703 (Scenic/Greenlee Dr.) matching the filing. She also holds ~26 mineral-rights interests in Travis County. Identity is well-corroborated; industry classed as Real Estate on the strength of the Rancho Viejo real-estate entities (rancher is secondary). Strong Democratic donor (FEC lean 1.0, 116 gifts).

organizations.role: President (oil & gas exploration and production company)
organizations.notes: Maralo LLC's own site lists Clayton Maebius as President under CEO Mary Ralph Lowe; the firm is an oil & gas company founded on Ralph Lowe's late-1930s oil exploration, drilling its first well in 1940. Maralo's office address is 1717 W. 6th St. Ste. 470, Austin, TX 78703 — matching the donor's zip 78703 exactly — and the filing's employer strings ('Maralo, LLC','Maralo LLC') and occupation ('Investments') align. The State Bar of Texas lists Clayton Yost Maebius with firm 'Maralo LLC', Austin, licensed 11/02/2001, now inactive and reporting 'Non-Law-Related Employment', consistent with running the oil & gas company rather than practicing. Notable: gave to Austin council member Paige Ellis and Kirk Watson; 3 FEC gifts at 0.0 partisan lean (uniformly Republican federally).
